chapter_title.ar: باب وَقْتِ الصَّلَاةِ فِي السَّفَرِ وَ الْجَمْعِ بَيْنَ الصَّلَاتَيْنِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 76 – Timing of the Salāt during the journey and the gathering between the two Salāt

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/372)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/372)
text: صَلَّيْتُ خَلْفَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عِنْدَ الزَّوَالِ فَقُلْتُ بِأَبِي وَ أُمِّي وَقْتُ الْعَصْرِ فَقَالَ وَقْتُ مَا تَسْتَقِيلُ إِبِلَكَ فَقُلْتُ إِذَا كُنْتُ فِي غَيْرِ سَفَرٍ فَقَالَ عَلَى أَقَلَّ مِنْ قَدَمٍ ثُلُثَيْ قَدَمٍ وَقْتُ الْعَصْرِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Abu Nasr, from Safwan Al-Jammal who said,

‘I prayed Salāt behind Abu Abdullahasws at midday, and I said, ‘By my father and my mother! (What is) the time for Al-Asr (Salāt)?’ So heasws said: ‘The time when you kneel your camel’. So I said, ‘When it was during other than a journey?’ So heasws said: ‘Upon less than one step (to) less than two-thirds of a step (shadow), it is time for Al-Asr’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Safwan al-Jammal who has said the following: “Once I performed Salah (prayer) behind abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, at the time of Zawal (declining of the sun toward the west at noontime) and I asked, saying, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, when is it time for al-‘Asr Salah (prayer)?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘It is when you release your camel.’ I then asked, ‘When it (time of al-‘Asr) is when I am not on a journey?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘It is time for al-‘Asr when it (the shadow of an object) is less than a step or it is two-thirds of a step.’”
